name: At the park
goal: Learn vocabulary related to animals and actions

dialogues.speaker: Tutor
dialogues.text: Do you have any pets?

dialogues.speaker: Student
dialogues.text: Yes, I have a dog.

dialogues.speaker: Tutor
dialogues.text: What do you usually do with your dog in the park?

dialogues.speaker: Student
dialogues.text: I walk and play with him every morning.

exercises.text: I usually ___ my dog in the park.
exercises.hint: Verb for taking a dog outside

exercises.text: Which of these is an animal?

name: At the restaurant
goal: Practice ordering food and talking about meals

dialogues.speaker: Tutor
dialogues.text: What kind of food do you like?

dialogues.speaker: Student
dialogues.text: I love Italian food.

dialogues.speaker: Tutor
dialogues.text: Do you often go to restaurants?

dialogues.speaker: Student
dialogues.text: Yes, I go out for dinner once a week.

exercises.text: I ___ a lot of fruit every day.
What do you call the midday meal?

dialogues.speaker: Tutor
dialogues.text: Where would you like to travel next?

dialogues.speaker: Student
dialogues.text: I want to visit Japan.

dialogues.speaker: Tutor
dialogues.text: Great choice! Have you bought your ticket yet?

dialogues.speaker: Student
dialogues.text: Not yet, I'm still planning.

exercises.text: You need a ___ to enter another country.
Where do planes take off and land?

name: Talking about work
goal: Practice professional vocabulary and describing your job

dialogues.speaker: Tutor
dialogues.text: What do you do for work?

dialogues.speaker: Student
dialogues.text: I work in a software company.

dialogues.speaker: Tutor
dialogues.text: What are your main responsibilities?

dialogues.speaker: Student
dialogues.text: I develop mobile applications.

exercises.text: I have an important ___ with my boss tomorrow.
What do you call the money you earn from your job?
The project deadline is next Friday.

name: Describing the weather
goal: Learn weather vocabulary and how to describe climate

dialogues.speaker: Tutor
dialogues.text: What is the weather like today?

dialogues.speaker: Student
dialogues.text: It is sunny and warm.

dialogues.speaker: Tutor
dialogues.text: Do you prefer hot or cold weather?

dialogues.speaker: Student
dialogues.text: I prefer warm weather. I don't like the cold.

exercises.text: In winter it is very ___ and sometimes it snows.
What do you use to measure temperature?
